title: daviesia incrassata subsp. incrassata
intro: Daviesia incrassata subsp. incrassata, also known as the rough Daviesia, is a plant species native to Western Australia, belonging to the Fabaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The rough Daviesia has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and alleviating pain, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional.
